methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Vital Lyfe is a seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Jonathan Criss and Andrew Harner that has raised $24M to date. The analysis relies on a single applicable valuation method due to the absence of publicly traded or mature private comparables in the consumer tech sector at this stage.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $24M total capital raised, reflecting typical valuation step-ups observed in seed-to-next-stage financings. This approach yields an implied valuation of $60M. The methodology assumes that invested capital serves as a reasonable proxy for current enterprise value at an early stage where revenue or profitability benchmarks are not yet available for comparison.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median point estimate of $60M. The relatively narrow band reflects the single-method constraint; reliance on stage progression alone introduces material uncertainty around the appropriate multiplier in the absence of comparable transaction data. Key risks to this valuation include execution risk inherent to seed-stage ventures, sensitivity to market conditions affecting follow-on funding availability, and the dependence on the 2.5x multiplier assumption. Obtaining additional comparable data or revenue/traction metrics would materially improve confidence in this range.
